title: We stopped clicking, and AI became the Internet
description: A provocative essay examining how the shift from human-driven to AI-driven web traffic is fundamentally changing the internet's nature—not killing it, but quietly reshaping it in ways designers need to understand. With over half of web traffic now generated by bots, this piece challenges assumptions about designing for users in 2026.

title: For most of my career I designed the cockpit
description: John Maeda explores the emerging field of AX (Agent Experience) design, drawing parallels from his cockpit design background to explain how designers must align AI capabilities with human intent. This foundational essay redefines design's role in an AI-first world, offering crucial perspective for anyone working at the intersection of AI and UX.

title: Exploring vs Exploiting
description: Ant Murphy breaks down the two critical modes of product discovery—exploration and exploitation—providing a mental model for product leaders and designers to balance innovation with optimization. Essential reading for teams deciding between pursuing new opportunities and perfecting existing ones.

title: Design for pain: how to make the worst moment better
description: A deeply human-centered case study on designing pain management interfaces for pediatric patients, exploring how UX can reduce anxiety and distress during medical procedures. This practical exploration of empathetic design demonstrates why context-aware, emotion-focused UX matters in healthcare.

title: Discovery vs Delivery
description: Buzz Usborne offers a personal reflection on the growing divide between deciding what should exist (discovery) and efficiently building it (delivery), with sharp observations about AI's impact on product quality. A thought-provoking take on how this split is reshaping product teams and design practice.

title: Boneyard: skeleton screens for your UI
description: A practical new tool that auto-extracts pixel-perfect skeleton loading screens from your real DOM with zero configuration, solving the common problem of layout shift during loading states. Useful for designers and developers looking to improve perceived performance without manual work.

title: Building glass for the web
description: A technical design case study on implementing a cross-browser glass morphism effect for web components, showing how visual trends evolve from platform conventions into reusable design systems. Valuable for design engineers exploring how to maintain visual consistency across browsers.

title: UI skills for design engineers
description: A curated resource platform for design engineers covering accessibility, motion, frontend craft, and interface quality—bridging the gap between design and implementation. A practical reference for teams building the next generation of hybrid design-engineering roles.
